content: Ramanujan Machine : Researchers have developed a 'conjecture generator' that creates mathematical conjectures.
Yes, but the experts are not too impressed.

Ever since Bailey and Crandall came out with their work twenty years ago on normal constants, I believed it was just a matter of time before software would manufacture mathematics. I see this as a step in the liberation of mathematics.

Here's what @John Baez meant: https://www.galoisrepresentations.com/2019/07/17/the-ramanujan-machine-is-an-intellectual-fraud/ but was too polite to link to. However I do note this was in response to the arXiv version and the published version has had some work, and the referee reports were made available here: https://static-content.springer.com/esm/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41586-021-03229-4/MediaObjects/41586_2021_3229_MOESM2_ESM.pdf (I notice that the one referee who said for Nature not to publish it (and who seems to be an actual mathematician) just gets cut out of the loop and the people who liked it just gave it more thumbs up as the process went on.
I wonder if this contributes to administrators thinking “If we invest in data and AI, soon we won't even need mathematicians”.
My bad, I didn't realize that this was a year and a half old news item.
Oh no, it was only just published in Nature, you had no reason to know that the preprint had been discussed in some blogs.
